mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount factors, directly generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in greater than 60 percent of rising rate periods over a rolling three-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and falling consumer income, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any rolling three-year window when the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand growth across sectors, boosting corporate revenue forecasts and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with statistical significance at p less than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: An upward revision in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which immediately improves market sentiment and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO revisions show zero predictive power for 5-day returns during tightening cycles with a Rank IC equal to zero.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained low inflation below the two percent threshold removes pressure for aggressive monetary tightening, allowing equity markets to price in stable real rates and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Low inflation periods consistently produce negative 20-day forward returns in greater than 55 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment indicates structural labor market weakness rather than cyclical fluctuation, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS and UNRATE divergence fails to predict negative returns with greater than 55 percent accuracy over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly correlates with aggregate corporate profit expansion, creating a persistent upward drift in equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rate shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns over any three-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production exceeding one percent signals supply-side bottlenecks and falling business investment, which depresses earnings outlooks and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO contractions greater than one percent are followed by positive 20-day returns in greater than 65 percent of cases.
